name: ADA Standards for Accessible Design 2010

jurisdiction.adopting_body: U.S. Department of Justice (28 CFR Part 36 App. B/D)

source_authority: U.S. Department of Justice (DOJ) / U.S. Access Board

is_alteration.label: Alteration to existing facility
is_alteration.description: Triggers ADA §202 alteration scoping (path-of-travel obligations to primary function areas, etc.).

is_public_use.label: Public-use facility
is_public_use.description: Title II (state/local government) and Title III (public accommodations) generally require full ADA compliance; private facilities not used by the public have narrower requirements.

rules.label: Accessible Route – Clear Width
rules.citation: ADA 2010 §402.2 – Accessible routes shall have a minimum clear width of 36 in. Passing spaces of 60 in. × 60 in. are required every 200 ft where route is < 60 in. wide.
rules.remediation: Increase accessible route width to minimum 36 in. clear. Where the route cannot achieve 60 in. wide, provide 60 × 60 in. passing spaces at intervals not exceeding 200 ft.

rules.label: Turning Space – 60-Inch Diameter Circle
rules.citation: ADA 2010 §304.3 – Turning spaces shall comply with either a 60 in. diameter circular space or a T-shaped space with 36 in. wide legs and 60 in. clearance.
rules.remediation: Provide a 60-in. diameter turning space in each accessible room, toilet room, and at the end of each accessible corridor. Alternatively, provide a T-shaped turning space per §304.3.2.

rules.label: Accessible Door – Clear Width
rules.citation: ADA 2010 §404.2.3 – Doorways shall provide a clear opening of not less than 32 in. measured between the face of the door and the opposite stop, with the door open 90°.
rules.remediation: Replace door and/or frame to achieve minimum 32 in. clear opening width at 90° door position. Consider offset hinges or wider door units to achieve compliance.

rules.label: Door Maneuvering Clearance
rules.citation: ADA 2010 §404.2.4 – Maneuvering clearances shall be provided at each side of the door: front approach/latch side: 18 in. min; depth at push side: 12 in. for doors with closers and latches.
rules.remediation: Provide adequate maneuvering clearance at accessible doors. Front latch-side approach requires 18 in. minimum clearance beyond the latch. Recess doors or relocate adjacent walls/fixtures.

rules.label: Ramp Slope
rules.citation: ADA 2010 §405.2 – Ramp running slope shall not exceed 1:12 (8.33%). Cross slope shall not exceed 1:48 (2.08%). Minimum clear width 36 in.
rules.remediation: Redesign ramp to achieve maximum 1:12 running slope and 1:48 cross slope. If the grade change cannot be accommodated at 1:12, provide an elevator or platform lift.

rules.label: Ramp Edge Protection
rules.citation: ADA 2010 §405.9 – Ramps shall have edge protection: a curb or barrier to prevent slip-off on each side not bounded by a wall or guardrail. Minimum curb height 4 in.
rules.remediation: Install 4-in. minimum curb on each open side of the ramp. Alternatively, extend the ramp surface beyond the edge or install a rail system that prevents a cane from slipping off.

rules.label: Accessible Parking Spaces – Count
rules.citation: ADA 2010 §208.2 / §502.2 – Number of accessible parking spaces per Table 208.2. 1 van-accessible space required per 6 accessible spaces.
rules.remediation: Provide the required number of accessible parking spaces per ADA Table 208.2, including the required proportion of van-accessible spaces (min 8 ft wide access aisle, 11 ft wide stall).

rules.label: Accessible Parking – Stall Dimensions
rules.citation: ADA 2010 §502.2 – Car accessible stall: 96 in. wide minimum. Van accessible stall: 132 in. wide minimum OR stall 96 in. wide with 96-in. access aisle.
rules.remediation: Restripe accessible parking to meet minimum stall dimensions. Car stalls: 96 in. wide with 60-in. access aisle. Van stalls: 132 in. wide with 60-in. aisle OR 96-in. wide with 96-in. aisle.

rules.label: Toilet Room – Turning Space
rules.citation: ADA 2010 §603.2.1 – Toilet rooms shall have a turning space complying with §304: a 60-in. diameter circle or T-shaped space. The turning space shall be within the toilet room.
rules.remediation: Reconfigure toilet room layout to provide a 60-in. diameter turning space entirely within the room (not overlapping with the door swing).

rules.label: Toilet Clearance
rules.citation: ADA 2010 §604.3 – Clearance around water closets: 60 in. on the transfer side (side approach), 48 in. in front. Toilet centerline: 16–18 in. from side wall.
rules.remediation: Reconfigure toilet room to provide 60-in. clear width on the transfer side and 48-in. clear depth in front of the toilet. Position toilet centerline 16–18 in. from the side wall.

rules.label: Forward Reach Range
rules.citation: ADA 2010 §308.2 – Forward reach: 15–48 in. AFF without obstruction. With obstruction ≤ 34 in. high, forward reach: 15–48 in. AFF.
rules.remediation: Relocate or adjust controls, dispensers, and other operable components to fall within the 15–48 in. forward reach range above finish floor.

rules.label: Handrail Gripping Surface
rules.citation: ADA 2010 §505.4 – Handrail gripping surfaces shall have a circular cross section 1.25–2 in. in diameter, or non-circular with perimeter 4–6.25 in. and max cross-section 2.25 in. Clearance from wall: 1.5 in. min.
rules.remediation: Replace handrail to meet ADA gripping surface requirements. Install round profile rails 1.25–2 in. diameter with minimum 1.5-in. clear space between rail and wall/brackets.

rules.label: Floor Surface – Carpet
rules.notes_template: Verify carpet on accessible routes is securely attached, has firm cushion (or no pad), and pile height ≤ 1/2 in. with exposed edges fastened and trim ≤ 1/2 in. high beveled.
rules.citation: ADA 2010 §302.2 – Carpet on a ground or floor surface shall be securely attached, have a firm cushion, pad, or backing, or no cushion or pad, and have a level loop, textured loop, level cut pile, or level cut/uncut pile texture. Pile height shall be 1/2 in. maximum.
rules.remediation: Specify low-pile (≤ 1/2 in.) carpet with firm or no pad, securely attached at all edges; trim transitions per §303.

rules.label: Changes in Level
rules.notes_template: Verify changes in level: ≤ 1/4 in. allowed without treatment; 1/4–1/2 in. shall be beveled at 1:2 max; > 1/2 in. requires a ramp, curb ramp, elevator, or platform lift.
rules.citation: ADA 2010 §303 – Changes in level of 1/4 in. high maximum shall be permitted to be vertical. Changes in level between 1/4 in. high minimum and 1/2 in. high maximum shall be beveled with a slope not steeper than 1:2.
rules.remediation: Eliminate or treat all level changes on accessible routes. Bevel 1/4–1/2 in. transitions at 1:2; use a ramp/elevator for anything taller.

rules.label: Clear Floor or Ground Space
rules.notes_template: Verify clear floor space at each operable element is ≥ 30 × 48 in. with one full unobstructed side adjacent to an accessible route, including knee/toe clearance per §306 where required.
rules.citation: ADA 2010 §305.3 – The clear floor or ground space shall be 30 in. minimum by 48 in. minimum.
rules.remediation: Provide a 30 × 48 in. clear floor space at each fixture, control, and feature requiring access, positioned for forward or parallel approach.

rules.label: Knee & Toe Clearance
rules.notes_template: Verify toe clearance: 9 in. high × 17–25 in. deep. Knee clearance: 27 in. high × 11 in. deep at 9 in. AFF, reducing to 8 in. deep at 27 in. AFF.
rules.citation: ADA 2010 §306 – Toe clearance: 9 in. high. Knee clearance: 27 in. high minimum at the leading edge, reducing to 11 in. min depth at 9 in. AFF and 8 in. min depth at 27 in. AFF.
rules.remediation: Detail knee/toe clearance under accessible counters, lavatories, drinking fountains, and other forward-approach fixtures to satisfy §306.

rules.label: Protruding Objects
rules.notes_template: Verify protruding objects: between 27 and 80 in. AFF protrude no more than 4 in. into the circulation path. Vertical clearance ≥ 80 in. (≥ 27 in. min cane-detection guard required where < 80 in.).
rules.citation: ADA 2010 §307 – Objects with leading edges more than 27 in. and not more than 80 in. above the finish floor or ground shall protrude 4 in. maximum horizontally into the circulation path.
rules.remediation: Reposition wall-mounted objects (e.g., extinguishers, signage, drinking fountains) so leading edges between 27 and 80 in. AFF do not protrude > 4 in. into the path; provide cane-detection guards where required.

rules.label: Operable Parts
rules.notes_template: Verify operable parts (faucets, switches, locks, hardware) are operable with one hand, without tight grasping, pinching, or twisting of the wrist, with a force ≤ 5 lbf, and located within reach ranges (§308).
rules.citation: ADA 2010 §309.4 – Operable parts shall be operable with one hand and shall not require tight grasping, pinching, or twisting of the wrist. The force required shall be 5 pounds (22.2 N) maximum.
rules.remediation: Specify lever-style hardware, push-plate or sensor faucets, and rocker/paddle switches; verify operating force ≤ 5 lbf for all operable parts on accessible routes.

rules.label: Walking Surfaces – Slope & Width
rules.notes_template: Verify accessible walking surfaces: running slope ≤ 1:20 (5%), cross slope ≤ 1:48 (~2%), clear width ≥ 36 in. (32 in. allowed for ≤ 24 in. length).
rules.citation: ADA 2010 §403 – Walking surfaces that are part of an accessible route shall have a running slope not steeper than 1:20 and a cross slope not steeper than 1:48; clear width 36 in. minimum.
rules.remediation: Re-grade or re-stripe accessible walking surfaces to meet slope and width minimums; treat anything > 1:20 running slope as a ramp under §405.

rules.label: Door Thresholds
rules.notes_template: Verify door thresholds ≤ 1/2 in. high and beveled at 1:2 max above 1/4 in.; existing or altered exterior sliding doors may be 3/4 in. max.
rules.citation: ADA 2010 §404.2.5 – Thresholds, if provided at doorways, shall be 1/2 in. high maximum. Raised thresholds and changes in level at doorways shall comply with §302 and §303.
rules.remediation: Specify thresholds ≤ 1/2 in. high, beveled at 1:2 max above 1/4 in., or replace with flush thresholds.

rules.label: Door Hardware
rules.notes_template: Verify door handles, pulls, latches, locks, and other operable hardware: lever, push-plate, or U-shaped; mounted 34–48 in. AFF; operable with one hand without tight grasping, pinching, or twisting.
rules.citation: ADA 2010 §404.2.7 – Handles, pulls, latches, locks, and other operable parts on accessible doors shall comply with §309.4. Operable parts of such hardware shall be 34 in. minimum and 48 in. maximum above the finish floor or ground.
rules.remediation: Specify lever or push-plate hardware mounted 34–48 in. AFF on every door on an accessible route.

rules.label: Door Closing Speed
rules.notes_template: Verify door closers: minimum 5 seconds from 90° to 12° open. Spring hinges: ≥ 1.5 seconds from 70° to closed.
rules.citation: ADA 2010 §404.2.8 – Door and gate closing speed: door closers and gate closers shall be adjusted so that from an open position of 90 degrees, the time required to move the door to a position of 12 degrees from the latch is 5 seconds minimum.
rules.remediation: Adjust closers to 5 s minimum sweep period; verify spring hinges meet 1.5 s minimum.

rules.label: Door Opening Force
rules.notes_template: Verify opening force: interior hinged doors and sliding/folding doors ≤ 5 lbf. Fire doors are required to have minimum opening force allowable by the AHJ (no upper ADA limit).
rules.citation: ADA 2010 §404.2.9 – The force required to push or pull open a door or gate other than a fire door shall be 5 pounds (22.2 N) maximum.
rules.remediation: Adjust closers and door hardware so non-fire-rated doors require ≤ 5 lbf to open.

rules.label: Ramp Rise & Landings
rules.notes_template: Verify each ramp run: maximum rise 30 in. between landings; landings ≥ 60 in. long, full ramp width; landings at 90° turns ≥ 60 × 60 in.
rules.citation: ADA 2010 §405.6 / §405.7 – The rise for any ramp run shall be 30 in. maximum. Landings shall be 60 in. minimum in the direction of travel.
rules.remediation: Break ramp runs to ≤ 30 in. rise with intermediate landings. Provide 60 in. minimum landings at top, bottom, and any direction change (60 × 60 at 90° turns).

rules.label: Ramp Handrails
rules.notes_template: Verify handrails on both sides of any ramp with a rise > 6 in.: top of gripping surface 34–38 in. above ramp surface; continuous within and between runs; 12-in. extensions top and bottom.
rules.citation: ADA 2010 §405.8 / §505 – Ramp runs with a rise greater than 6 in. shall have handrails complying with §505 on both sides.
rules.remediation: Add handrails to both sides of ramp runs with rise > 6 in.; verify 34–38 in. height, continuity, and 12-in. extensions per §505.10.

rules.label: Elevator Operation & Signals
rules.notes_template: Verify accessible elevators per ASME A17.1 and §407: hall call buttons (centerline 42 in. AFF, ≥ 3/4 in. dia), hall lanterns (visible+audible), car controls (Braille+raised characters, 35–48 in. AFF), audible/visual signals, and reopening sensors.
rules.citation: ADA 2010 §407 – Passenger elevators shall comply with §407 (hall calls, hall signals, hoistway signs, car interior, doors, leveling, illumination, controls, designations, audible & visual signals, emergency communication).
rules.remediation: Specify §407-compliant elevator package: hall calls/lanterns, accessible car controls (with Braille and tactile characters), reopening sensors, ≤ 1/2-in. leveling, illuminated controls, two-way emergency communication.

rules.label: Platform Lifts
rules.notes_template: Platform lifts permitted only in specific scoping conditions (§206.7); when used, comply with ASME A18.1 and provide unassisted entry/operation/exit and 36-in. door clear width.
rules.citation: ADA 2010 §410 – Platform lifts shall comply with ASME A18.1 (Safety Standard for Platform Lifts and Stairway Chairlifts).
rules.remediation: Provide ASME A18.1-compliant lift, unassisted entry/exit, 36-in. door clear width, and audible/visual signals; confirm scoping under §206.7 permits use of a lift.

rules.label: Passenger Loading Zones
rules.notes_template: Verify any passenger loading zone has a 60-in. wide × 20-ft long access aisle adjacent and parallel to the vehicle space, with vertical clearance ≥ 114 in. along the vehicle/aisle/route to the entrance.
rules.citation: ADA 2010 §503 – Passenger loading zones shall provide an access aisle 60 in. wide minimum and 20 ft long minimum, adjacent and parallel to the vehicle pull-up space.
rules.remediation: Stripe a 60 × 240 in. access aisle adjacent to the loading zone with 114-in. vertical clearance along the route to the entrance.

rules.label: Stairs – Treads, Risers, Nosings
rules.notes_template: Verify stairs (where required to be accessible): tread depth 11 in. min, riser height 4–7 in., uniform within ± 3/8 in., nosings beveled or radiused (≤ 1/2 in. projection), open risers prohibited.
rules.citation: ADA 2010 §504 – All steps on a flight of stairs shall have uniform riser heights and uniform tread depths. Risers shall be 4 in. high minimum and 7 in. high maximum.
rules.remediation: Detail stair geometry to satisfy §504; use closed risers or comply with the open-riser exception, and detail nosings per §504.5.

rules.label: Handrail Extensions
rules.notes_template: Verify handrail extensions: at top of stair, ≥ 12 in. horizontal beyond top riser; at bottom, one tread depth + sloped continuation; on ramps, ≥ 12 in. horizontal beyond top and bottom of run.
rules.citation: ADA 2010 §505.10 – Handrail gripping surfaces shall extend beyond and in the same direction of stair flights and ramp runs in accordance with §505.10.1–.3.
rules.remediation: Detail handrail extensions per §505.10 at top and bottom of every stair flight and ramp run.

rules.label: Drinking Fountains
rules.notes_template: Verify drinking fountains: provide both a wheelchair-accessible unit (spout 36 in. max AFF, knee clearance, 30 × 48 forward approach) and a standing-height unit (spout 38–43 in. AFF). 50% of each type required to be accessible per §211.
rules.citation: ADA 2010 §602 / §211 – Drinking fountains shall comply with §602. At least 50% of the drinking fountains provided on each floor shall be located on an accessible route, and accessible drinking fountains shall meet wheelchair and standing-height criteria.
rules.remediation: Provide hi-lo bi-level fountains (36-in. wheelchair + 38–43-in. standing) on each floor, located on accessible routes.

rules.label: Toilet Grab Bars
rules.notes_template: Verify grab bars at water closets: side wall bar ≥ 42 in. long starting ≤ 12 in. from rear wall and extending ≥ 54 in. from rear wall; rear wall bar ≥ 36 in. long centered with extensions per §604.5.2; mounted 33–36 in. AFF.
rules.citation: ADA 2010 §604.5 – Grab bars for water closets shall be provided on the side wall closest to the water closet and on the rear wall in accordance with §604.5.1 and §604.5.2.
rules.remediation: Provide compliant side and rear grab bars at every accessible water closet; confirm reinforcement in walls.

rules.label: Lavatories & Sinks
rules.notes_template: Verify lavatories: rim ≤ 34 in. AFF; clear floor space 30 × 48 with forward approach; knee/toe clearance per §306; insulated/protected hot water and drain pipes; faucets per §309.
rules.citation: ADA 2010 §606 – Lavatories shall have a clear floor space, rim 34 in. high maximum, knee and toe clearance, insulated water supply and drain pipes, and accessible controls.
rules.remediation: Specify accessible lavatory at 34-in. rim with insulated supply/drain piping, sensor or lever faucet, and required clearances.

rules.label: Shower Compartments
rules.notes_template: Verify shower compartments: transfer-type 36 × 36 in. with 36 × 48 clearance, or roll-in 30 × 60 in. minimum; grab bars per §608.3; controls per §608.5; thresholds ≤ 1/2 in.; hand-held spray per §608.6.
rules.citation: ADA 2010 §608 – Shower compartments shall be transfer type, standard roll-in type, or alternate roll-in type per §608.2 and shall comply with §608.3 (grab bars), §608.4 (seats), §608.5 (controls), §608.6 (spray unit), §608.7 (thresholds), and §608.8 (enclosures).
rules.remediation: Detail an accessible shower of the appropriate type with grab bars, controls, hand-held spray on a 59-in. flexible hose, and ≤ 1/2-in. threshold.

rules.label: Signs
rules.notes_template: Verify accessible signage: tactile signs at permanent room IDs (raised 1/32-in. characters, Grade 2 Braille, mounted 48–60 in. to baseline of lowest line, on latch side); visual signs sized for viewing distance per §703.5.
rules.citation: ADA 2010 §703 – Signs shall comply with §703 (tactile, visual, pictogram, Braille, installation, and symbols of accessibility).
rules.remediation: Specify §703 tactile signs at permanent room IDs (raised + Braille) and §703.5 visual signs sized for viewing distance; locate signs per §703.4.

rules.label: Medical Care & Long-Term Care Facilities
rules.notes_template: Verify medical-facility scoping (§223): 10% of patient sleeping rooms accessible (general); 100% in rehabilitation; 50% in long-term care. Each accessible patient room shall comply with §805 (clearances, toilet rooms, communication features).
rules.citation: ADA 2010 §805 / §223 – Medical care and long-term care facilities shall provide accessible patient sleeping rooms in the proportion required by §223 and shall comply with §805 (turning space, doors, toilet rooms, clear floor space at fixtures, and communication features).
rules.remediation: Build accessible patient-room schedule per §223 ratios and detail each accessible room to satisfy §805 turning space, fixture clearances, and communication features.

rules.label: Dining Surfaces & Work Surfaces
rules.notes_template: Verify dining/work surfaces: tops 28–34 in. AFF, knee/toe clearance per §306, clear floor space per §305 with forward approach. 5% of fixed dining tables (and ≥ 1) accessible per §226.
rules.citation: ADA 2010 §902 / §226 – At least 5% of dining surfaces and work surfaces shall be accessible. Tops shall be 28 in. minimum and 34 in. maximum above the floor.
rules.remediation: Designate ≥ 5% (and ≥ 1) of fixed dining/work tables as accessible (28–34 in. tops, knee/toe clearance, 30 × 48 forward approach).

rules.label: Amusement Rides
rules.notes_template: If amusement rides are present, verify accessible routes to ride load/unload areas and comply with §1002 (wheelchair spaces, transfer seats, transfer devices, signage).
rules.citation: ADA 2010 §1002 – Amusement rides shall provide accessible routes, wheelchair spaces, transfer seats, or transfer devices in accordance with §234.
rules.remediation: Engage an accessibility consultant; document compliance with §234 scoping and §1002 technical.
